creator: gahr@FreeBSD.org
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA512


Rodrigo OSORIO (ros) wrote:
[snip]
| I take a look for the problem you report and after read the install
| manual and made some tests, I can conclude there is no default
| "omegat.prefs" file created during install.
|
| When you run the application for the first time, the ~/.omegat directory
| is created with only a log.txt file in.
| But when you close the application the default omegat.prefs file is
| create for the user.
| After than there is no more warnings.
[snip]

Ok, but how do I start the application in order to let it be closed and
create the file? I can't, since it throws exception and exits before
starting.

Would it be a solution to include a standard/default omegat.prefs in the
port? Maybe you can take your own, anonymize/generalize it, and include
it as extra-patch or something.

| Rodrigo OSORIO

- --
Pietro Cerutti
